findings.title: Malformed 'convergence of … → 0' phrasing
findings.snippet: Convergence of $u'(t) \to 0$ follows from the energy identity and boundedness.
findings.explanation: "Convergence of X → 0" conflates two ways of expressing a limit: "convergence of X to 0" and "X → 0". The preposition 'of' expects a noun phrase, not a full limit statement.
findings.fix: That $u'(t) \to 0$ follows from the energy identity and boundedness.
